ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general dosa entry (FOODON_03302051); kal dosai is a thicker stone-griddle variant not separately coded. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FKP covers dosa as a category; kal dosai (thick stone-griddle variant) is not separately enumerated. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept exists for kal dosai or dosa as a food product at this time.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard restaurant serving of kal dosai consists of two thick, fluffy pancakes weighing approximately 200 g in total, typically served with sambar and coconut chutney.

nutrient_source.title: Dosa (plain, fermented rice and lentil pancake) — ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: ICMR-NIN reports plain dosa per 100 g. Kal dosai is a thicker, stone-griddle variant made from the same fermented rice-urad dal batter; macronutrient profile is closely analogous. Values adapted from ICMR-NIN plain dosa entry. Serving size set to 200 g (2 pieces × ~100 g each) based on typical restaurant portion.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of fermented rice-lentil foods: dosa — University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008
gi_source.source_match_notes: The University of Sydney GI Database lists plain dosa (fermented rice and urad dal batter, griddle-cooked) with a GI of approximately 51 (low). Kal dosai uses the same batter composition and fermentation process; the thicker profile does not materially alter GI. GI of 51 applied as closest analogous estimate. GL per serving = (51 × 29.5 × 200/100) / 100 = 30.1.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~210 mg per 100 g; ~420 mg per 2-piece serving)
hypertension.guidance: A 2-piece serving contributes approximately 420 mg sodium (~18% of the 2300 mg daily limit); individuals with hypertension should monitor total sodium intake across the meal, particularly if sambar or pickles are consumed alongside.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~30 per 2-piece serving) despite low GI (51)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Although kal dosai has a low GI of 51, the 200 g serving delivers approximately 59 g of carbohydrate and a glycemic load of ~30, which is high; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size to one piece and pair with high-fibre accompaniments such as sambar to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat and zero cholesterol; plant-based fermented food
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Kal dosai is low in saturated fat (~0.6 g per 100 g) and contains no cholesterol, making it a heart-friendly carbohydrate source when consumed in moderate portions and without high-fat accompaniments such as ghee or coconut oil.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kal dosai (கல் தோசை) is a thick, fluffy South Indian fermented pancake cooked on a traditional stone griddle (kal means 'stone' in Tamil), distinguishing it from the thinner, crispier plain dosa. The batter is made by soaking and wet-grinding parboiled rice and urad dal (black gram lentils) in a ratio of approximately 3:1, then fermenting overnight to develop a mild sour flavour and a light, airy texture. The fermentation process increases B-vitamin bioavailability and reduces phytic acid, improving mineral absorption. Kal dosai is cooked on a seasoned stone or cast-iron griddle with a small amount of oil, producing a soft, spongy interior and a lightly browned exterior. In Singapore, it is a popular breakfast and tiffin item at Indian restaurants and hawker stalls, typically served in pairs with sambar (a lentil-vegetable soup) and coconut chutney. Nutritionally, kal dosai is a moderate-energy, low-fat, plant-based staple providing approximately 168 kcal per 100 g, with 4.5 g protein, 3.7 g fat, and 29.5 g carbohydrate. It is naturally gluten-free, vegan, and halal. The glycemic index is approximately 51 (low), though the glycemic load per 2-piece serving is high (~30), making portion control important for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. Sodium content is moderate and should be considered in hypertension management.
common_user_queries: How many calories in kal dosai?
Is kal dosai good for diabetics?
What is the difference between kal dosai and plain dosa?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN plain dosa data, which uses the same fermented rice-urad dal batter. Kal dosai's thicker profile does not materially alter macronutrient composition per 100 g. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for plain dosa. No exact kal dosai entry exists in any approved primary source.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN plain dosa (fermented rice and urad dal pancake); kal dosai uses the same batter and fermentation process. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not separately reported in the ICMR-NIN dosa entry. 3. polyunsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not separately reported in the ICMR-NIN dosa entry. 4. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero for a plant-based, minimally processed fermented food with no hydrogenated fats. 5. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN dosa entry. 6. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN dosa entry. 7. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN dosa entry. 8. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN dosa entry. 9. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN dosa entry. 10. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN dosa entry. 11. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; kal dosai is a plant-based food containing no animal-derived ingredients. 12. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per ICMR-NIN dosa entry; fermented rice-lentil batter contains negligible vitamin C. 13. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero; kal dosai is a plant-based food with no animal-derived ingredients and B12 is naturally zero. 14. GI of 51 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for plain dosa; kal dosai uses identical batter composition. 15. Serving size of 200 g (2 × ~100 g pieces) is based on typical Singapore restaurant portion. 16. FoodOn URI FOODON_03302051 and FoodEx2 code A0FKP are closest-parent matches for dosa; no kal dosai-specific ontology codes exist in current releases.
